headline: WIP Aging for pre-commit/pre-commit as of May 15, 2026: 6 in-flight items, 6 already past P85 (1.1d), 6 past P95 (1.1d).
definition: Each dot is one in-flight item, placed in the column of its current workflow state at a height equal to its Age (days since entering the workflow). Percentile lines come from the cycle times of recently completed items — read horizontally as risk thresholds: an item aging past P85 likely misses its forecast. See *When Will It Be Done?* by Daniel Vacanti.

top_interventions.title: use git cat-file -e instead of git rev-list in _rev_exists
fix: Proper way of finding `npm pack` `filename` output.

key_insight: 6 item(s) have aged past the P85 cycle time (1.1d) — Vacanti's threshold for likely forecast miss. Oldest: #3427, #3460, #3498. Concentrate decision-making here.
next_actions: 6 item(s) past P95 (1.1d): decide explicitly to expedite, split, or drop. They are now outliers in your own historical distribution.

Biggest WIP column is 'Awaiting Review' with 5 item(s). Investigate this stage — either work is arriving faster than it can leave, or items are waiting on a downstream resource (reviewers, environments, decisions).
Re-check daily; Aging is a leading indicator of forecast slip.
caveats: Aging is a snapshot as of May 15, 2026 — not a trend over time.
Percentile lines reflect the completed-item distribution from Apr 15, 2026 → May 14, 2026 (5 items). If recent throughput has changed, those checkpoints may have drifted.
Items in early workflow states (Open, To Do) often age longest in OSS — that's queue, not work. Treat the chart as a queue-depth indicator first, a productivity signal second.

100% of in-flight items are past P95 of recent completers — much higher than the ~5% you'd expect if today's WIP looked like recent history. The P85/P95 thresholds are derived only from items that have already finished, so long-running items still in flight aren't in that sample. The thresholds therefore diverge from — and likely under-represent — true cycle time for this WIP.

vocabulary.WIP (Work In Progress): Items currently in any state the team treats as in-progress — for this report, items whose current GitHub label is one of --wip-labels, or whose current Jira status is in --workflow.
vocabulary.Aging Work In Progress: How long each in-flight item has been open without exiting the workflow. Aging only applies to items that haven't completed; a completed item has a Cycle Time instead.
vocabulary.Work item age: Elapsed days since an item entered the workflow. Computed as today − createdAt for a PR; for Jira, today − issue creation.
vocabulary.In-flight items: Items that have entered but not exited the workflow. Each in-flight item is one dot on the Aging chart, in the column of its current state.
vocabulary.Cycle time percentile lines: Reference checkpoints drawn from the cycle times of recently completed items. If an in-flight item ages past P85, it's likely to miss its forecast — actionable evidence to intervene.
vocabulary.Aging chart: Plots in-flight items by current workflow state (x) and Age in days (y), with horizontal percentile lines drawn from recent completers' cycle times.

items.title: feature: fetch tag pointing to HEAD in shallow clone
use git cat-file -e instead of git rev-list in _rev_exists
fix: Proper way of finding `npm pack` `filename` output.

cli_invocation: uv run flow aging --repo pre-commit/pre-commit --asof 2026-05-15 --workflow 'Draft,Awaiting Review,Changes Requested,Approved' --history-start 2026-04-15 --history-end 2026-05-14
